message:
type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Implementing global filter registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding custom filters param to prompt decorator, from_str/from_file, create_jinja_env helper

type: QUESTION
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Are you adding a per-call/local 'filters' parameter to Prompt/from_str/from_file? My global registry needs local filters to take precedence over global ones, want to avoid clobbering your changes to _template_from_str/_template_from_file signatures.

type: INTENT
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding create_jinja_env(filters=None) helper used by from_str/from_file/prompt decorator; will register default filters there first, then merge in a filters dict param. If you add a global registry, please have create_jinja_env also merge global registry filters (before per-call filters override) so we don't clobber each other - happy to adjust order once you share your function names.

type: ANSWER
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: My registry: module-level _FILTER_REGISTRY dict, register_filter(name, func), unregister_filter(name). Currently I do env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY) before setting built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args) in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file. Please have create_jinja_env do: 1) env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY) (global, lowest precedence), 2) set the built-in filters, 3) env.filters.update(filters or {}) (per-call, highest precedence) - so per-call > builtins > global. I'll adapt _FILTER_REGISTRY usage to fit inside your create_jinja_env once you land it, just ping me when it's ready so we don't clobber each other's edits.

type: ANSWER
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Yes: I added filters:Optional[Dict[str,Callable]]=None to Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, Prompt._template_from_str, Prompt._template_from_file, and to the prompt decorator (now prompt(fn=None, filters=None) supporting both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...})). New create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) helper builds the env, registers builtin filters, then does env.filters.update(filters) last so per-call filters override. For your global registry, register those filters inside create_jinja_env BEFORE the per-call filters.update(filters) line so local filters still take precedence - I'm done editing now, feel free to layer your changes in create_jinja_env.

type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Done: added _FILTER_REGISTRY dict + register_filter()/unregister_filter() in outlines/prompts.py; merged into env.filters in both _template_from_str and _template_from_file (registry applied before built-ins, so built-ins can't be shadowed). Self-contained, doesn't depend on a shared create_jinja_env helper. Patch written to patch.txt.
